A female warrior stands atop or within a dense layer of white clouds that fills the lower third of the frame, the ground entirely invisible beneath her, suggesting she occupies a space above the world. The image is rendered in a desaturated palette of near-monochrome grey tones with only the faintest warmth in the mid-tones. She is shown in full profile facing left, her body in a dynamic archery stance, bow drawn fully taut with an arrow nocked and aimed off-frame to the left. Her left arm extends straight outward gripping the bow, while her right hand pulls the drawstring back to her jaw. Her long black hair streams violently to the right in strong wind, pulled nearly horizontal and merging visually with the tattered edges of a large dark cape or cloak that billows and shreds behind her in the same direction. The cloak appears to be constructed partially from feathers or has deteriorated into feathered fragments at its trailing edges, giving it an avian, almost wing-like quality in motion. Her armor is dark leather and wrapped textile, heavily layered at the torso and hips with what appear to be feathers, strips of hide, and straps at multiple points. Tall dark boots reach the knee. The sky above is a flat, pale grey with no cloud detail, creating a stark and empty backdrop that throws her dark silhouette into sharp contrast. The overall composition is diagonal and kinetic, the tension of the drawn bow and the horizontal sweep of hair and cloak creating strong opposing vectors across the frame.
Keywords: female archer, above the clouds, feathered armor, dark warrior, drawn bow, windswept hair, monochrome fantasy, tattered cloak

A young woman with long straight auburn and burgundy hair and striking pale blue eyes is photographed in a soft-focus background of pale icy blue-grey, her face receding into a shallow depth of field while her hands dominate the foreground in sharp detail. She extends both arms toward the viewer, palms upward and cupped slightly open, as though offering something unseen. Her hands and forearms are rendered entirely in translucent crystal or glass, perfectly formed in human anatomy but hollow and clear, revealing the warm amber and orange light glowing from within them. The internal light is intense at the palms and wrists, bleeding outward through the transparent material in warm firelight tones that contrast sharply against the cold blue of the background and her clothing. The glass surface of the hands catches fine surface texture and irregularities, giving them the appearance of formed ice or thick resin rather than polished crystal. Her body suit or jacket visible at the edges of the frame shares a similar reflective metallic quality, catching the same orange-gold glow from below. A narrow column of warm light rises from between her hands toward her throat and chin, connecting the foreground brightness to her softly lit face. Her expression is composed and direct, her blue eyes calm despite the fire she carries. The overall palette splits cleanly between cool ice tones in the upper two thirds and warm molten light in the lower third, creating a strong thermal contrast across the composition.

Her eyes meet the camera directly. Below the jaw, her body is encased in an extraordinary biomechanical exosuit that blends structural and organic forms in equal measure. At the neck, dark branching filaments rise like exposed tendons or circuitry, forming an intricate lattice collar that connects her human face to the structure below. The torso suit is translucent or semi-transparent in sections, with a grey-silver sheen that allows the warm undertones of the body beneath to show faintly through the material. Across the chest and sternum, dark veining spreads outward in a tree-root or vascular pattern, converging at a central vertical seam that runs from collarbone to waist. The shoulder joints are articulated with angular mechanical plating, and the arms are encased in the same dark structured material, segmented at the joints. The overall silhouette is narrow and elongated, with no excess bulk. The background is a heavily textured surface of layered grey-green paint with visible brushwork and patches of warm copper-gold bleeding through in irregular areas, giving the backdrop a painterly, aged quality that contrasts with the precision of the figure. The lighting is soft and even, with a slight warmth concentrated on the face and diffusing into cool tones across the suit.

record.text: JMIR Res Protocols: Assessing the Effectiveness of an Awareness Program on Knowledge and Practice Regarding the Mothers’ Absolute Affection Program Among Postnatal Mothers in Selected Hospitals: #Protocol for a Quasi-Experimental #Study

external.description: Background: The Mothers’ Absolute Affection (MAA) program is a national initiative launched by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India, on August 5, 2016, to promote, support, and protect breastfeeding across the country. The program aims to provide focused breastfeeding promotion and counseling services through the health system. It targets approximately 39 million pregnant and nursing mothers, 880,000 Accredited Social Health Activists, 150,000 subcenters, and 17,000 delivery points across all states and union territories. Despite its wide reach, there remains a lack of valid and reliable tools to assess mothers’ knowledge and practices related to breastfeeding and the MAA program. Objective: The aims of this #Study are to assess the existing knowledge and practice regarding the MAA program among postnatal mothers, to evaluate the effectiveness of an awareness program on knowledge and practice regarding the MAA program, to correlate knowledge and practice regarding the MAA program among postnatal mothers, and to associate knowledge and practice with selected demographic variables. Methods: A quasi-experimental one-group pretest and posttest design was used to evaluate the effectiveness of an awareness program for the MAA program among postnatal mothers. The #Study was conducted at a selected hospital with a sample of 80 postnatal mothers selected through inclusion and exclusion criteria. On Day 1, participants underwent a pretest followed by an awareness session on the MAA program. A posttest was administered on Day 2. Knowledge was assessed using a structured questionnaire consisting of 15 items, while practice was evaluated using a 5-point Likert scale. Data were analyzed using descriptive and inferential statistics, including paired tests and tests. Results: Knowledge scores were classified into three levels—poor (0%‐33.33%), fair (33.34%‐66.66%), and good (66.67%‐100%)—based on the number of correct responses out of 15. Practice was categorized as poor (0%‐50%) or good (51%‐100%) based on the 5-point scale score. The #Study revealed significant improvements in both knowledge and practice following the awareness intervention, indicating that the program was effective. The scoring procedure provided a comprehensive understanding of the awareness and practical implementation of the MAA program among postnatal mothers. Conclusions: The awareness program significantly improved the knowledge and practices of postnatal mothers regarding the MAA program, emphasizing the value of educational interventions in promoting breastfeeding. Trial Registration: Clinical Trials Registry India CTRI/2025/03/081926; https://ctri.nic.in/Clinicaltrials/pmaindet2.php?EncHid=MTIxMjMy&Enc=&userName= International Registered Report Identifier (IRRID): RR1-10.2196/73496
external.title: Assessing the Effectiveness of an Awareness Program on Knowledge and Practice Regarding the Mothers’ Absolute Affection Program Among Postnatal Mothers in Selected Hospitals: #Protocol for a Quasi-Experimental #Study
